title: POD-only persistent types
description: All types stored in persistent memory must be trivially copyable (POD). This ensures the entire memory image can be saved and loaded as raw bytes without serialization logic.
rationale: Trivially copyable types allow the pmm memory image to be persisted and restored via raw byte I/O, which is the fundamental persistence mechanism.
author: netkeep80
acceptance_criteria: No virtual functions, non-trivial constructors, or pointer members in persistent types
